name: Hoces del Cabriel Cuchillares and Moluengo Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de las Hoces del Cabriel — Circular desde Villargordo del Cabriel
description: This difficult 35.4-mile mountain biking route in Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park offers stunning gorges and geological formations.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villargordo Del Cabriel offers diverse and rugged terrain within the western part of the Valencia Region. The area is characterized by the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, where the Cabriel River carves dramatic gorges, valleys, and ravines. This landscape includes riverside forests, Mediterranean woodlands, and features like the Moluengo peak and the Contreras Reservoir, providing varied conditions for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Villargordo Del Cabriel The most popular mountain bike trail is Hoces del Cabriel Cuchillares and Moluengo Loop, a difficult 35.4 miles (56.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route showcases stunning gorges and geological formations within the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Enguídanos and Contreras Reservoir Loop from La Pesquera, a moderate 28.3 miles (45.6 km) path. This trail offers spectacular views of the Embalse de Contreras. Local mountain bikers also love the Peña Azul Viewpoint – El Moluengo loop from Venta del Moro, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail leading through natural parks and rugged terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Villargordo Del Cabriel is defined by dramatic gorges, the Cabriel River, and diverse forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging technical descents. The routes in Villargordo Del Cabriel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villargordo Del Cabriel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Villargordo Del Cabriel?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villargordo Del Cabriel offer a range of challenges. Out of 20 available routes, 12 are rated as &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; and 8 are considered &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. This means you'll find options for experienced riders seeking technical descents and demanding climbs, as well as routes suitable for those with a good level of fitness looking for a rewarding ride through diverse terrain.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Villargordo Del Cabriel?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Villargordo Del Cabriel are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910789747/hoces-del-cabriel-cuchillares-and-moluengo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel Cuchillares and Moluengo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017766015/enguidanos-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-la-pesquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enguídanos and Contreras Reservoir Loop from La Pesquera&lt;/a&gt; are both popular circular options.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, particularly within the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park. You can expect to encounter dramatic gorges carved by the Cabriel River, and enjoy stunning vistas from various viewpoints. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4475750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Azul Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/812619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Rabo de la Sartén Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel Valley Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The serene Contreras Reservoir is also a prominent feature along many routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villargordo Del Cabriel?
A: Villargordo Del Cabriel benefits from a climate with a predominance of sunny days, making it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying good weather. Winter can also be a good option, especially on sunnier days, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Villargordo Del Cabriel has historical significance. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4602462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Path Trench&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3867155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Trench&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the region's past. The town itself also has historical elements, including traditional architecture and hints of its Arabic heritage.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Villargordo Del Cabriel?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Villargordo Del Cabriel varies depending on their length and difficulty. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910789747/hoces-del-cabriel-cuchillares-and-moluengo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel Cuchillares and Moluengo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 35.4 miles (56.9 km), typically takes around 5 hours 20 minutes. A slightly shorter difficult route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villargordo del Cabriel – Cabriel Valley Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; (27.2 miles / 43.7 km), can be completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villargordo Del Cabriel?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villargordo Del Cabriel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 9 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse and rugged terrain, the stunning gorges, and the breathtaking views, particularly within the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park. The variety of trails catering to different skill levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any trails that offer technical sections for advanced riders?
A: Yes, the diverse terrain around Villargordo Del Cabriel includes options for advanced riders. Some routes feature challenging technical sections, offering an exhilarating experience. The difficult-rated trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21953263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Azul Viewpoint – El Moluengo loop from Venta del Moro&lt;/a&gt;, are known to provide more demanding riding experiences.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by water features like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Cabriel River is a central feature of the region, and many trails wind alongside it or offer views of its dramatic gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017766015/enguidanos-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-la-pesquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enguídanos and Contreras Reservoir Loop from La Pesquera&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides spectacular views of the Embalse de Contreras. The Cabriel River is also recognized as one of Europe's cleanest, adding to the scenic appeal.

Q: What kind of natural environment will I experience on the mountain bike trails?
A: You'll experience a rich tapestry of natural environments. The trails traverse the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, featuring riverside forests with poplars, willows, and tamarix, as well as Mediterranean species like pine, holm oak, and juniper in the forested areas. The landscape is characterized by dramatic gorges, valleys, and ravines carved by the Cabriel River, offering a truly immersive natural experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those seeking an extended ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910789747/hoces-del-cabriel-cuchillares-and-moluengo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel Cuchillares and Moluengo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 35.4-mile (56.9 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Azul Viewpoint – La Higuera - Cabriel River loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 33.5 miles (53.9 km), both offering significant distances and elevation changes.
tips: Old telegraph tower located in a site of great scenic, cultural and historical interest. Surrounded by very nice views of the Cabriel Valley and by the trenches and bunkers of the War of Independence.

Viewpoint with amazing views of the Cabriel Valley, the Contreras reservoir and the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, which stands out for its "blades" formed by the erosion of the Cabriel River over the years.
Very entertaining path and path that runs through the El Ventorro ravine in a very pleasant and picturesque setting

Town created during the construction of the Contreras dam (1950-1974) that today is practically abandoned. It is a very striking place for those curious about ghost places, where you can find abandoned and forgotten buildings with schools, bars, sports centers and others. Undoubtedly a recommended site for those who like to visit abandoned towns.

Bridge built in the 19th century under the reign of Elizabeth II. Also known as the "centipede" since it was originally intended as a suspension bridge but due to the change in typology it ended up having innumerable columns that gave it this nickname. Later it was rebuilt in its current form with 7 concrete arches.

This rock formation is located within the Hoces del Cabriel natural park. The way in which the valley opens is very striking and allows you to see the cliffs that, due to their structure and the effects of erosion, create peaks that look like knives, hence the name Cuchillar de Contreras.
Road in excellent condition that runs through the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park and with very nice views towards the Cuchillares de Contreras
Gravel road in perfect condition that runs through very picturesque landscapes of the province of Cuenca, passing through crops and rustic farms.

Located on the Cabriel River, it unites the provinces of Cuenca and Valencia. Its construction dates back to the 16th century and still retains its original form. A bridge of great cultural, historical and scenic interest that also marks the beginning-end of the Turia-Cabriel Nature Trail.

With its more than 121 km of route, the Turia – Cabriel Nature Trail manages to unite two of the most important rivers in the province of Valencia: the Turia and the Cabriel. This itinerary is protected by two natural parks: the Turia Natural Park and the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park.
This particular section begins (or ends) at the Vadocañas bridge over the Cabriel River. It is a gravel road in excellent condition with gentle slopes and surrounded by vegetation. Ideal for cycling or touring it by mtb or gravel bike.

Top of the peak "El Moluengo" at 1023 m above sea level, highest point of the Natural Park of Las Hoces del Cabriel. It allows to appreciate beautiful views to all the valley of the Cabriel, the province of Valencia and the one of Cuenca.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route typically takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete. However, actual time may vary based on your fitness level, riding skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a "difficult" mountain bike ride, requiring "very good fitness" and "advanced riding skills." It features varied and technical sections, and some segments may even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting from Villargordo del Cabriel. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, Villargordo del Cabriel is the logical starting point, and you should be able to find parking within the village.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and challenging. You'll encounter a mix of forest tracks, some asphalt sections, and potentially more technical segments. Be prepared for varied surfaces, as some parts of the wider Hoces del Cabriel area can be demanding, occasionally requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers numerous highlights, including the historical El Romeroso Telegraph Tower and the stunning Peñas Blancas Viewpoint, which provides breathtaking views of the Cabriel Valley. You'll also pass through Contreras Village, cross the Contreras Bridge - Cabriel River, and witness the impressive geological formations of Cuchillares de Contreras. Other notable features include the Vadocañas Bridge, and sections of the crystal-clear Cabriel River.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Turia-Cabriel Nature Trail, the Camí Natural Turia - Cabriel, Camino Natural del Turia - Cabriel, Camino de la Fonseca, and the Sendero de Los Cuchillos del Cabriel, among others.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging nature of this mountain bike route and the presence of diverse wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult ride.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking within the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park. However, as it is a protected area, always respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the demanding ride more challenging, while winter might bring colder conditions. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for tackling this difficult route?
faqs.answer: Given its "difficult" rating and requirement for "advanced riding skills," be prepared for varied terrain, including potential technical sections and segments where you might need to push your bike. Ensure your mountain bike is in excellent condition, and carry sufficient water, snacks, and repair tools. The route's length and elevation gain demand very good fitness, so pace yourself and enjoy the stunning views from points like Peñas Blancas Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near Villargordo del Cabriel?
faqs.answer: Villargordo del Cabriel, as the starting point, offers some local amenities. You can typically find accommodation options, cafes, and restaurants in the village to refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.
